text:
Market today: Mixed but with real bright spots. Micron's massive earnings beat (revenue quadrupled) is the headline that matters most for your book — memory crunch pricing is sending a strong signal that chip demand is holding up. Qualcomm popping adds to the semi tailwind. Offset by Iran war spending and geopolitical noise keeping risk appetite choppy.

Your holdings: Three need eyes. PLTR is down 12.77% and flagged SELL — on margin, that hole gets expensive fast, no stop trigger yet but watch it. AVGO is your largest position, down 12.45% and flagged TRIM — the semi news today is friendly but you're still in the red on your biggest bet; consider sizing down into any bounce. SPAL is a 2x leveraged ETF, already down 14.48% — decay and leverage eat you alive if it drifts; tight leash. SPCX is brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no track record — margin amplifies any gap-down hard.

Watch: 1) Does AVGO respond to today's Micron/Qualcomm semi strength with a real bounce? That's your trim-or-hold trigger. 2) PLTR price action — any break toward that $85 stop.

Bottom line: Your biggest risk today is AVGO sizing on margin — use any semi-sector pop to reduce exposure before the position manages you.
